title: glandularia multiglandulosa
intro: Glandularia multiglandulosa, also known as the many-flowered verbena, is a perennial plant species native to South America, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and multi-branched stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, coughs, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Glandularia multiglandulosa include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized for various health purposes.
